body.title: agent_datacenter: Postgres shim — pg_ctl lifecycle, write/read/rollback self-test
body.result: PostgresShim in devices/postgres/shim.py. pg_ctl start/stop/restart (Docker fallback). self_test write/read/rollback in _rack_self_test table (rolled back, no permanent state). rollback() kills pg + removes pid file.
body.description:
First concrete shim implementation. Owns Postgres lifecycle and provides write/read/rollback self-test.

Proposed shape:
- agent_datacenter/devices/postgres/shim.py:
  class PostgresShim(BaseShim):
    device_id = 'postgres'
    start() → subprocess.run(['pg_ctl', 'start', '-D', data_dir]); wait for ready; return bool
    stop() → subprocess.run(['pg_ctl', 'stop', '-m', 'fast', '-D', data_dir]); return bool
    restart() → stop() then start()
    self_test() → connect; INSERT INTO _rack_self_test (ts) VALUES (now()); SELECT; DELETE; COMMIT
      returns {passed: True, details: 'write/read/rollback OK, latency: Xms'}
    rollback() → kill pg process; remove pid file if startup fails mid-way
  Docker fallback: if pg_ctl not found, use docker exec pg_container pg_ctl ... pattern
  self_test creates _rack_self_test table if not exists; uses its own isolated schema

Affected files:
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/devices/postgres/shim.py

Design rules:
- no-sqlite: shim manages Postgres; no sqlite3 anywhere
- docs-in-code: comment on Docker fallback decision path

Scope boundary:
IN: start/stop/restart via pg_ctl (Docker fallback), self_test write/read/rollback, rollback() for failed starts
OUT: Postgres device self-description (T-adc-postgres-device), block behavior propagation (T-adc-postgres-block-clear-error)

Test plan:
- PostgresShim().self_test() against live Postgres returns {passed: True}
- self_test() creates no permanent state (table cleaned up after test)
- stop() → start() round-trip succeeds
- rollback() called when start() raises: pg process terminated, pid file removed
